title: Finding angle measures using triangles
description: Use your knowledge about angles to find missing angle measures in various complex situations.
topic_path: Get ready for congruence, similarity, and triangle trigonometry

assessment_items.question_content:
In the following diagram,  $\overline{BC}$ is parallel to $\overline{DE}$.

**What is the measure of $\blueD{\angle x}$?**  
*Angles are not necessarily drawn to scale.*

 

[[☃ image 1]]



$\blueD{\angle x}$ $=$ [[☃ numeric-input 1]]$\Large{{}^\circ}$
hints:
The interior angles of a triangle always add up to $180^\circ$.

$\begin{align}\greenD{\angle ABC} + \purpleD{\angle BAC} +\maroonD{\angle BCA} &= 180^\circ
\\\\
\greenD{54^\circ}+\purpleD{105^\circ} + \maroonD{\angle BCA}&= 180^\circ
 \\\\
\maroonD{\angle BCA} &= 21^\circ \end{align}$



[[☃ image 1]] 

Since $\overline{BC}$ is parallel to $\overline{DE}$, $\maroonD{\angle BCA}$ and $ \blueD{\angle{DAF}}$ are corresponding angles, so they are congruent.

$\blueD{\angle DAF}$ is the same as $\blueD{\angle x}.$

So  $\maroonD{\angle BCA}$ and $ \blueD{\angle{x}}$ $ = \blueD{21^\circ}$.



[[☃ image 1]]

options.alt: Two parallel lines labeled line BC and line DE.  Line FC intersects the parallel lines at point A and C.  Line segment AB connects the parallel lines.  Points B and D are on the same side of line FC. Angle ABC is 54 degrees.  Angle BAC is 105 degrees. Angle DAF is x degrees.

\\\\
\greenD{42^\circ}+\purpleD{103^\circ} + \maroonD{\angle BCA}&= 180^\circ
 \\\\
\maroonD{\angle BCA} &= 35^\circ \end{align}$


[[☃ image 1]]

Since $\overline{BC}$ is parallel to $\overline{DE}$, $\maroonD{\angle BCA}$ and $ \blueD{\angle{DAF}}$ are corresponding angles, so they are congruent.

$\blueD{\angle DAF}$ is the same as $\blueD{\angle x}.$

So  $\maroonD{\angle BCA}$ and $ \blueD{\angle{x}}$ $ = \blueD{35^\circ}$.

assessment_items.question_content:
In the following diagram,  $\overline{BC}$ is parallel to $\overline{DE}$.

**What is the measure of $\blueD{\angle x}$?**  
*Angles are not necessarily drawn to scale.*


[[☃ image 1]]



$\blueD{\angle x}$ $=$ [[☃ numeric-input 1]]$\Large{{}^\circ}$
hints:
The interior angles of a triangle always add up to $180^\circ$.

$\begin{align}\greenD{\angle ABC} + \purpleD{\angle BAC} +\maroonD{\angle BCA} &= 180^\circ
\\\\
\greenD{42^\circ}+\purpleD{109^\circ} + \maroonD{\angle BCA}&= 180^\circ
 \\\\
\maroonD{\angle BCA} &= 29^\circ \end{align}$



[[☃ image 1]]

Since $\overline{BC}$ is parallel to $\overline{DE}$, $\maroonD{\angle BCA}$ and $ \blueD{\angle{CAE}}$ are alternate interior angles, so they are congruent.

$\blueD{\angle CAE}$ is the same as $\blueD{\angle x}.$

So  $\maroonD{\angle BCA}$ and $ \blueD{\angle{x}}$ $ = \blueD{29^\circ}$.



[[☃ image 1]]

options.alt: Two parallel lines labeled line BC and line DE.  Line FC intersects the parallel lines at point A and C.  Line segment AB connects the parallel lines.  Points B and D are on the same side of line FC. Angle ABC is 42 degrees.  Angle BAC is 109 degrees. Angle EAC is x degrees.

So  $\maroonD{\angle BCA}$ and $ \blueD{\angle{x}}$ $ = \blueD{37^\circ}$.

assessment_items.question_content:
In the following diagram,  $\overline{BC}$ is parallel to $\overline{DE}$.

**What is the measure of $\blueD{\angle x}$?**  
*Angles are not necessarily drawn to scale.*


[[☃ image 1]]



$\blueD{\angle x}$ $=$ [[☃ numeric-input 2]]$\Large{{}^\circ}$
hints:
The interior angles of a triangle always add up to $180^\circ$.

$\begin{align}\greenD{\angle ABC} + \purpleD{\angle BAC} +\maroonD{\angle BCA} &= 180^\circ
\\\\
\greenD{42^\circ}+\purpleD{105^\circ} + \maroonD{\angle BCA}&= 180^\circ
 \\\\
\maroonD{\angle BCA} &= 33^\circ \end{align}$



[[☃ image 1]]

Since $\overline{BC}$ is parallel to $ \overline{DE}$, $\maroonD{\angle BCA}$ and $ \blueD{\angle{CAE}}$ are alternate interior angles, so they are congruent.

$\blueD{\angle CAE}$ is the same as $\blueD{\angle x}.$

So  $\maroonD{\angle BCA}$ and $ \blueD{\angle{x}}$ $ = \blueD{33^\circ}$.

assessment_items.question_content:
**What is the measure of $\blueD{\angle x}$?**  
*Angles are not necessarily drawn to scale.*

 
[[☃ image 1]]



$\blueD{\angle x}$ $=$ [[☃ numeric-input 2]] $\Large{{}^\circ}$
hints:
The interior angles of a triangle always add up to $180^\circ$.

$\begin{align}\greenD{\angle BDH} + \maroonD{\angle DBH}  + \purpleD{\angle DHB}   &= 180^\circ
\\\\
\greenD{39^\circ}+\maroonD{36^\circ} + \purpleD{\angle DHB}&= 180^\circ
 \\\\
\purpleD{\angle DHB} &= 105^\circ \end{align}$


[[☃ image 1]]

$\purpleD{\angle DHB}$ and $\blueD{\angle x}$ are supplementary angles because they form a straight line.

$\begin{align}\purpleD{\angle DHB} + \blueD{\angle x} &= 180^\circ
\\\\
\purpleD{105^\circ}+\blueD{\angle x} &= 180^\circ
 \\\\
\blueD{\angle x} &= 75^\circ \end{align}$


[[☃ image 1]]

options.alt: A figure made up of 5 line segments that form a star shape. Line segment AC, line segment CD, line segment DB, line segment BE, and line segment EA. Angle B is 36 degrees. Angle D is 39 degrees. Angle EHI is x degrees.

assessment_items.question_content:
**What is the measure of $\blueD{\angle x}$?**  
*Angles are not necessarily drawn to scale.*


[[☃ image 1]]



$\blueD{\angle x}$ $=$ [[☃ numeric-input 1]] $\Large{{}^\circ}$
hints:
The interior angles of a triangle always add up to $180^\circ$.

$\begin{align}\greenD{\angle BDH} + \maroonD{\angle DBH}  + \purpleD{\angle DHB}   &= 180^\circ
\\\\
\greenD{31^\circ}+\maroonD{40^\circ} + \purpleD{\angle DHB}&= 180^\circ
 \\\\
\purpleD{\angle DHB} &= 109^\circ \end{align}$


[[☃ image 1]]

$\purpleD{\angle DHB}$ and $\blueD{\angle x}$ are supplementary angles because they form a straight line.

$\begin{align}\purpleD{\angle DHB} + \blueD{\angle x} &= 180^\circ
\\\\
\purpleD{109^\circ}+\blueD{\angle x} &= 180^\circ
 \\\\
\blueD{\angle x} &= 71^\circ \end{align}$


[[☃ image 1]]

options.alt: A figure made up of 5 line segments that form a star shape. Line segment AC, line segment CD, line segment CB, line segment BE, and line segment EA. Angle B is 40 degrees. Angle D is 31 degrees. Angle EHI is x degrees.

**What is the measure of $\blueD{\angle x}$?**  
*Angles are not necessarily drawn to scale.*

[[☃ image 1]]



$\blueD{\angle x}$ $=$ [[☃ numeric-input 2]] $\Large{{}^\circ}$
hints:
The interior angles of a triangle always add up to $180^\circ$.

$\begin{align}\greenD{\angle BDH} + \maroonD{\angle DBH}  + \purpleD{\angle DHB}   &= 180^\circ
\\\\
\greenD{33^\circ}+\maroonD{46^\circ} + \purpleD{\angle DHB}&= 180^\circ
 \\\\
\purpleD{\angle DHB} &= 101^\circ \end{align}$



[[☃ image 1]]

$\purpleD{\angle DHB}$ and $\blueD{\angle x}$ are supplementary angles because they form a straight line.

$\begin{align}\purpleD{\angle DHB} + \blueD{\angle x} &= 180^\circ
\\\\

 \\\\

assessment_items.question_content:
**What is the measure of $\blueD{\angle x}$?**  
*Angles are not necessarily drawn to scale.*


[[☃ image 1]]



$\blueD{\angle x}$ $=$ [[☃ numeric-input 2]] $\Large{{}^\circ}$
hints:
$\purpleD{\angle DIJ}$ and $\maroonD{\angle AIC}$ are supplementary angles because they form a straight line.

$\begin{align}\purpleD{\angle DIJ} + \maroonD{\angle JIC} &= 180^\circ
\\\\
\purpleD{\angle DIJ}+\maroonD{ 105^\circ} &= 180^\circ
 \\\\
\purpleD{\angle DIJ} &= 75^\circ \end{align}$



[[☃ image 1]]

The interior angles of a triangle always add up to $180^\circ$.

$\begin{align}\greenD{\angle JDI} +  \purpleD{\angle DIJ} + \goldD{\angle DJI}   &= 180^\circ
\\\\
\greenD{39^\circ}+ \purpleD{75^\circ} + \goldD{\angle DJI} &= 180^\circ
 \\\\
\goldD{\angle DJI}  &= 66^\circ \end{align}$


[[☃ image 1]]




$\goldD{\angle DJI}$ and $ \blueD{\angle{x}}$ are vertical angles, so they are congruent.

$\goldD{\angle DJI}$ and $\blueD{\angle{x}}$ $ = \blueD{66^\circ}$


[[☃ image 1]]

options.alt: A figure made up of 5 line segments that form a star shape. Line segment AC, line segment CD, line segment CB, line segment BE, and line segment EA. Angle JIH is 105 degrees. Angle D is 39 degrees. Angle AJF is x degrees.

assessment_items.question_content:
**What is the measure of $\blueD{\angle x}$?**  
*Angles are not necessarily drawn to scale.*


[[☃ image 1]]



$\blueD{\angle x}$ $=$ [[☃ numeric-input 2]] $\Large{{}^\circ}$
hints:
$\purpleD{\angle DIJ}$ and $\maroonD{\angle AIC}$ are supplementary angles because they form a straight line.

$\begin{align}\purpleD{\angle DIJ} + \maroonD{\angle JIC} &= 180^\circ
\\\\
\purpleD{\angle DIJ}+\maroonD{ 107^\circ} &= 180^\circ
 \\\\
\purpleD{\angle DIJ} &= 73^\circ \end{align}$


[[☃ image 1]]

The interior angles of a triangle always add up to $180^\circ$.

$\begin{align}\greenD{\angle JDI} +  \purpleD{\angle DIJ} + \goldD{\angle DJI}   &= 180^\circ
\\\\
\greenD{31^\circ}+ \purpleD{73^\circ} + \goldD{\angle DJI} &= 180^\circ
 \\\\
\goldD{\angle DJI}  &= 76^\circ \end{align}$


[[☃ image 1]]





$\goldD{\angle DJI}$ and $ \blueD{\angle{x}}$ are vertical angles, so they are congruent.

$\goldD{\angle DJI}$ and $\blueD{\angle{x}}$ $ = \blueD{76^\circ}$


[[☃ image 1]]

options.alt: A figure made up of 5 line segments that form a star shape. Line segment AC, line segment CD, line segment CB, line segment BE, and line segment EA. Angle JIH is 107 degrees. Angle D is 31 degrees. Angle AJF is x degrees.

assessment_items.question_content:
In the following diagram   $\overline{DE} \parallel \overline{FG}$
 and $\overline{KL} \perp \overline{FG}$.

**What is the measure of $\blueD{\angle x}$?**  
*Angles are not necessarily drawn to scale.* Given the following:






[[☃ image 1]]



$\blueD{\angle{x}} = $ [[☃ numeric-input 1]] $\Large{{}^\circ}$
hints:
$\purpleD{\angle{BAC}}$ and $ \green{\angle{IAK}}$ are vertical angles.

So  $\purpleD{\angle{BAC}} =  \green{\angle{IAK}}$.



[[☃ image 1]]



$\overline{KL} \perp \overline{FG}$, so $\goldD{\angle{ABC}} = \goldD{90^\circ}$.


[[☃ image 1]]

The interior angles of a triangle always add up to $180^\circ$.


$\begin{align}\purpleD{\angle BAC} + \goldD{\angle ABC} +\maroonD{\angle{BCA}} &= 180^\circ
\\\\
\purpleD{58^\circ}+\goldD{90^\circ} + \maroonD{\angle{BCA}}&= 180^\circ
 \\\\
\maroonD{\angle{BCA}} &= 32^\circ \end{align}$






[[☃ image 1]]






$\maroonD{\angle{BCA}}$ and $ \blueD{\angle{x}}$ are vertical angles, so they are congruent.

$\maroonD{\angle{BCA}}$ and $\blueD{\angle{x}}$ $ = \blueD{32^\circ}$



[[☃ image 1]]

options.alt: Two parallel lines labeled line DE and line FG.  Line KL intersects the parallel lines at points A and B.  Line IJ intersects the parallel lines at points A and C.  Angle IAK is 58 degrees.  Angle GCJ is x degrees.

The interior angles of a triangle always add up to $180^\circ$.

\\\\
\purpleD{62^\circ}+\goldD{90^\circ} + \maroonD{\angle{BCA}}&= 180^\circ

[[☃ image 1]]

$\maroonD{\angle{BCA}}$ and $ \blueD{\angle{x}}$ are vertical angles, so they are congruent.

assessment_items.question_content:
In the following diagram, $\overline{HI}$ is parallel to $\overline{JK}$.

**What is the measure of $\blueD{\angle x}$?**  
*Angles are not necessarily drawn to scale.*






[[☃ image 1]]

$\blueD{\angle{x}} = $ [[☃ numeric-input 1]]  $\Large{{}^\circ}$
hints:
Since $\overline{HI}$ is parallel to $\overline{JK}$, $\goldD{\angle{AJK}}$ and $ \green{\angle{AHI}}$ are corresponding angles.

So  $\goldD{\angle{AJK}} = \green{\angle{AHI}}$.



[[☃ image 1]]

The interior angles of a triangle always add up to $180^\circ$.

$\begin{align}\purpleD{\angle JAK} + \goldD{\angle AJK} +\blueD{\angle x} &= 180^\circ
\\\\
\purpleD{66^\circ}+\goldD{56^\circ} + \blueD{\angle x}&= 180^\circ
 \\\\
\blueD{\angle x} &= 58^\circ \end{align}$



[[☃ image 1]]

options.alt: Two parallel lines labeled line JK and line HI.  Line AJ intersects the parallel lines at points H and J.  Line AK intersects the parallel lines at points I and K.  Angle HAI is 66 degrees.  Angle AHI is 56 degrees. Angle AKJ is x degrees.

hints:
Since $\overline{HI}$ is parallel to $\overline{JK}$, $\goldD{\angle{AJK}}$ and $ \green{\angle{AHI}}$ are corresponding angles.

So  $\goldD{\angle{AJK}} = \green{\angle{AHI}}$.

[[☃ image 1]]

The interior angles of a triangle always add up to $180^\circ$.

$\begin{align}\purpleD{\angle JAK} + \goldD{\angle AJK} +\blueD{\angle x} &= 180^\circ
\\\\
\purpleD{59^\circ}+\goldD{61^\circ} + \blueD{\angle x}&= 180^\circ
 \\\\
\blueD{\angle x} &= 60^\circ \end{align}$


[[☃ image 1]]

options.alt: Two parallel lines labeled line JK and line HI.  Line AJ intersects the parallel lines at points H and J.  Line AK intersects the parallel lines at points I and K.  Angle HAI is 59 degrees.  Angle AHI is 61 degrees. Angle AKJ is x degrees.

assessment_items.question_content:
In the following diagram  $\overline{HI} \parallel \overline{JK}$.

**What is the measure of $\blueD{\angle x}$?**  
*Angles are not necessarily drawn to scale.*



[[☃ image 1]]

$\blueD{\angle{x}} = $ [[☃ numeric-input 2]]  $\Large{{}^\circ}$
hints:
Since $\overline{HI}$ is parallel to $\overline{JK}$, $\goldD{\angle{AHI}}$ and $ \green{\angle{AJK}}$ are corresponding angles.

So  $\goldD{\angle{AHI}} = \green{\angle{AJK}}$. 



[[☃ image 1]]

The interior angles of a triangle always add up to $180^\circ$.

$\begin{align}\purpleD{\angle HAI} + \goldD{\angle AHI} +\blueD{\angle x} &= 180^\circ
\\\\
\purpleD{46^\circ}+\goldD{67^\circ} + \blueD{\angle x}&= 180^\circ
 \\\\
\blueD{\angle x} &= 67^\circ \end{align}$


[[☃ image 1]]

options.alt: Two parallel lines labeled line JK and line HI.  Line AJ intersects the parallel lines at points H and J.  Line AK intersects the parallel lines at points I and K.  Angle HAI is 46 degrees.  Angle AIH is x degrees. Angle AJK is 67 degrees.

assessment_items.question_content:
In the following diagram  $\overline{HI} \parallel \overline{JK}$.

**What is the measure of $\blueD{\angle x}$?**  
*Angles are not necessarily drawn to scale.*



[[☃ image 1]]

$\blueD{\angle{x}} = $ [[☃ numeric-input 2]]  $\Large{{}^\circ}$
hints:
Since $\overline{HI}$ is parallel to $\overline{JK}$, $\goldD{\angle{AHI}}$ and $ \green{\angle{AJK}}$ are corresponding angles.

So  $\goldD{\angle{AHI}} = \green{\angle{AJK}}$. 



[[☃ image 1]]

The interior angles of a triangle always add up to $180^\circ$.

$\begin{align}\purpleD{\angle HAI} + \goldD{\angle AHI} +\blueD{\angle x} &= 180^\circ
\\\\
\purpleD{45^\circ}+\goldD{63^\circ} + \blueD{\angle x}&= 180^\circ
 \\\\
\blueD{\angle x} &= 72^\circ \end{align}$

 
[[☃ image 1]]

options.alt: Two parallel lines labeled line JK and line HI.  Line AJ intersects the parallel lines at points H and J.  Line AK intersects the parallel lines at points I and K.  Angle HAI is 45 degrees.  Angle AIH is x degrees. Angle AJK is 63 degrees.

assessment_items.question_content:
In the following diagram  $\overline{HI} \parallel \overline{JK}$.

**What is the measure of $\purpleD{\angle x}$?**  
*Angles are not necessarily drawn to scale.*


[[☃ image 1]]

$\purpleD{\angle{x}} = $ [[☃ numeric-input 2]] $\Large{{}^\circ}$
hints:
Since $\overline{HI}$ is parallel to $\overline{JK}$, $\goldD{\angle{AIH}}$ and $ \blueD{\angle{AKJ}}$ are corresponding angles.

So  $\goldD{\angle{AIH}} = \blueD{\angle{AKJ}}$.


[[☃ image 1]]

The interior angles of a triangle always add up to $180^\circ$.

$\begin{align}\greenD{\angle AHI} + \goldD{\angle AIH} +\purpleD{\angle x} &= 180^\circ
\\\\
\greenD{66^\circ}+\goldD{62^\circ} + \purpleD{\angle x}&= 180^\circ
 \\\\
\purpleD{\angle x} &= 52^\circ \end{align}$


[[☃ image 1]]

options.alt: Two parallel lines labeled line JK and line HI.  Line AJ intersects the parallel lines at points H and J.  Line AK intersects the parallel lines at points I and K.  Angle HAI is x degrees.  Angle AHI is 66 degrees. Angle AKJ is 62 degrees.

options.alt: Two parallel lines labeled line JK and line HI.  Line AJ intersects the parallel lines at points H and J.  Line AK intersects the parallel lines at points I and K.  Angle HAI is x degrees.  Angle AHI is 56 degrees. Angle AKJ is 60 degrees.

assessment_items.question_content:
**What is the measure of $\blueD{\angle x}$?**  
*Angles are not necessarily drawn to scale.*




[[☃ image 1]]



$\blueD{\angle x} = $ [[☃ numeric-input 1]] $\Large{{}^\circ}$
hints:
$\greenD{\angle DAB}$ and $\goldD{\angle BAC}$ are supplementary angles because they form a straight line.

$\begin{align}\greenD{\angle DAB} + \goldD{\angle BAC} &= 180^\circ
\\\\
\greenD{114^\circ}+\goldD{\angle BAC} &= 180^\circ
 \\\\
\goldD{\angle BAC} &= 66^\circ \end{align}$



 

[[☃ image 1]]

The interior angles of a triangle always add up to $180^\circ$.

$\begin{align}\purpleD{\angle ABC} + \goldD{\angle BAC} +\blueD{\angle x} &= 180^\circ
\\\\
\purpleD{56^\circ}+\goldD{66^\circ} + \blueD{\angle x}&= 180^\circ
 \\\\
\blueD{\angle x} &= 58^\circ \end{align}$





[[☃ image 1]]

options.alt: Triangle ABC with 1 exterior angle. Line segment AD is on the outside of the triangle and angle DAB makes the exterior angle. Angle DAB is 114 degrees. Angle ABC is 56 degrees. Angle BCA is x degrees.

 \\\\

assessment_items.question_content:
**What is the measure of $\greenD{\angle x}$?**  
*Angles are not necessarily drawn to scale.*





[[☃ image 1]]



$\greenD{\angle x} = $ [[☃ numeric-input 2]]  $\Large{{}^\circ}$
hints:
The interior angles of a triangle always add up to $180^\circ$.

$\begin{align}\purpleD{\angle ABC} + \goldD{\angle BAC} +\blueD{\angle ACB} &= 180^\circ
\\\\
\purpleD{69^\circ}+\goldD{\angle BAC} + \blueD{66^\circ}&= 180^\circ
 \\\\
\goldD{\angle BAC} &= 45^\circ \end{align}$




[[☃ image 1]]

$\greenD{\angle DAB}$ and $\goldD{\angle BAC}$ are supplementary angles because they form a straight line.

$\greenD{\angle DAB}$ is the same as $\greenD{\angle x}.$

$\begin{align}\greenD{\angle x} + \goldD{\angle BAC} &= 180^\circ
\\\\
\greenD{\angle x}+\goldD{45^\circ} &= 180^\circ
 \\\\
\greenD{\angle x}&= 135^\circ \end{align}$



[[☃ image 1]]

\greenD{\angle x}&= 138^\circ \end{align}$
